batch_rule: Production-planning artifact only. Each script brief must be transformed, checked, sourced, and approved before any upload or public use.

script_briefs.title: The CEO Whose First Job Is Saying No
script_briefs.hook: The first thing this AI CEO did was not launch anything. It built brakes.
outline: Many agents can create noise faster than value.
The CEO role allocates lanes, reads evidence, and preserves gates.
The first proof added AI Resources, human-action feed, YouTube lane, and customer intake.
Control plane first, public launch later.
title_variants: I built a CEO for an AI agent company, and its first job was saying no
Why my AI agent company needed brakes before workers
The boring control layer that makes AI agents useful
script_briefs.thumbnail_brief: Split-screen command board versus chaos board. Text: CEO FIRST.
script_briefs.risk_notes: Do not imply revenue has been earned. Use local artifacts as proof.

script_briefs.title: The Fastest Way To Test An AI Tool Is Not To Install It
script_briefs.hook: Most tools fail before install. You just need the right test.
outline: Check overlap with existing tools first.
Define first local proof.
Check license, runtime, account, API cost, and data gates.
Promote, watch, or reject with reason.
title_variants: The fastest AI tool test happens before install
Stop installing every shiny AI repo
My two-block rule for testing AI tools
script_briefs.thumbnail_brief: Stopwatch, repo card, red/green local proof stamp. Text: TEST FIRST.
script_briefs.risk_notes: No claims about specific third-party tools without current source review.

script_briefs.title: Local Proof Beats Public Launch
script_briefs.hook: A local proof is the cheapest way to find out if a launch is worth it.
outline: Public launch creates reputation and account risk.
Local artifacts reveal feasibility and quality first.
Examples include product drafts, bounty checklists, and YouTube no-post batches.
Gate only after proof is strong.
title_variants: A local proof beats a public launch when agents move fast
Before you launch, make the proof cheap
Why I am building no-post assets first
script_briefs.thumbnail_brief: Small local file stack facing large upload button. Text: PROOF FIRST.
script_briefs.risk_notes: Avoid presenting local plans as market validation.

script_briefs.title: Workers Are Not Service Workers
script_briefs.hook: Some AI workers build. Some touch the world. Those should not be the same permission.
outline: Workers produce local artifacts.
Service workers prepare or execute gated actions.
External actions require exact approval.
This prevents accidental posts, trades, uploads, or account actions.
title_variants: How I split AI workers from service workers
The permission boundary every agent company needs
Why my AI workers cannot just post things
script_briefs.thumbnail_brief: Two labeled lanes: BUILD and TOUCH WORLD. Text: SPLIT THEM.
script_briefs.risk_notes: Do not imply service workers are currently executing live actions.

script_briefs.title: AI Resources: HR For Agents
script_briefs.hook: If a company has HR, an agent company needs AR: AI Resources.
outline: New agents are hires, not decorations.
AR checks overlap before adding tools.
AR runs local tests and fast-fails.
AR retires duplicate or stale agents.
title_variants: AI Resources: HR for agents, tools, and workflows
The department that stops AI sprawl
Why every agent company needs AI Resources
script_briefs.thumbnail_brief: Org chart with one highlighted department. Text: AI RESOURCES.
script_briefs.risk_notes: Avoid overclaiming that AR has tested all candidates already.

script_briefs.title: A YouTube Channel Can Start Before The Channel Exists
script_briefs.hook: The safest first YouTube step is not making the channel. It is making ten usable briefs.
outline: Channel creation has account and terms gates.
Uploading has public-action and copyright gates.
No-post batches test audience, format, and production load.
The first proof is scripts, titles, thumbnails, and manifest.
title_variants: A YouTube channel can start before you create the channel
The no-post YouTube test I use first
Before channel creation: build the content batch
script_briefs.thumbnail_brief: Empty channel page outline beside content cards. Text: NO-POST TEST.
script_briefs.risk_notes: No upload or channel creation instruction.

script_briefs.title: Every Money Idea Needs A Kill Rule
script_briefs.hook: A money idea without a kill rule is just a time sink wearing a costume.
outline: Define proof, gate, expected value, and timebox.
Kill when payout path is unclear.
Kill when gates dominate value.
Promote only when proof is strong.
title_variants: Why every money idea needs a kill rule
The anti-time-sink rule for online money experiments
How I stop AI agents from chasing bad ideas
script_briefs.thumbnail_brief: Timer, checklist, crossed-out weak idea. Text: KILL RULE.
script_briefs.risk_notes: Avoid financial advice; keep examples operational.

script_briefs.title: Turning A Prompt Pack Into A Product Without Listing It
script_briefs.hook: You can make a digital product more real without listing it anywhere.
outline: Package local assets.
Draft listing copy privately.
Check fees, terms, payout, and support burden.
Stop before account, payment, and public listing gates.
title_variants: Turning a prompt pack into a product without listing it
How to make a digital product real before a marketplace
Private product readiness beats rushed listings
script_briefs.thumbnail_brief: Product folder, private draft badge, marketplace gate. Text: NOT LISTED YET.
script_briefs.risk_notes: Do not imply marketplace approval or sales.

script_briefs.title: Big AI Prizes, Tiny First Proof
script_briefs.hook: A huge prize does not mean your first step should be huge.
outline: Competitions have rules, accounts, datasets, and submission gates.
First proof is a local baseline and eligibility packet.
Decide after proof whether to spend more time.
Avoid confusing prize size with expected value.
title_variants: Kaggle and ARC prizes look huge, but the first proof is tiny
The small first step before an AI competition
Do not chase a prize before a baseline
script_briefs.thumbnail_brief: Big trophy beside tiny local notebook. Text: TINY PROOF.
script_briefs.risk_notes: Do not mention active competition claims without current source verification.

script_briefs.title: Prediction Markets Are Research Until Treasury Says Otherwise
script_briefs.hook: A market hypothesis is not a trade. It is research until the treasury gate says yes.
outline: Public data can support paper research.
Real-money action needs eligibility, fees, liquidity, risk, and kill switch.
Paper evidence must precede capital.
The CEO should not confuse curiosity with permission.
title_variants: Prediction markets are research until the treasury gate says otherwise
A market idea is not a trade
Why paper evidence comes before real-money bets
script_briefs.thumbnail_brief: Paper ticket beside locked treasury door. Text: PAPER ONLY.
script_briefs.risk_notes: Not financial advice. No trading instruction.

b_roll_and_source_plan: Use only generated, self-created, or licensed visuals until public-action review approves sourced material.
Internal artifacts may be shown as blurred or redacted local documents.
Do not use platform logos, creator clips, YouTube thumbnails, repo screenshots, marketplace pages, or competition pages without source review.
Customer-provided videos should be routed through youtube_material_route_template_v1 and transformed into lessons; do not copy phrasing, structure, or visuals directly.
